title: Writing an academic essay
explanation:
Writing an academic essay

Academic essays at C1 level require a clear thesis, hedged claims and coherent argumentation.

  • Thesis statement in the introduction
  • Topic sentences in each paragraph
  • Evidence and cautious interpretation
  • Formal conclusion

questions.text: [*This essay contends that remote work reshapes urban economies in complex ways.|Essay contends remote work reshapes urban economies complex.|Remote work essay contends urban.]

[*It could be argued that flexibility benefits employees, but it may also blur work-life boundaries.|Could argued flexibility benefits employees blur boundaries.|Flexibility argued employees benefits.]
[*The evidence suggests, rather than proves, a link between commuting time and wellbeing.|Evidence proves definitely link commuting wellbeing.|Link evidence commuting proves.]
[*In conclusion, policymakers should treat remote work as a structural shift, not a temporary trend.|Conclusion policymakers remote work temporary trend.|Policymakers conclusion remote work.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Academic essays should avoid overly ____ claims. | *absolute | formal | precise
Each paragraph needs a clear topic ____.  | *sentence | picture | ticket
Use reporting verbs such as argue, suggest and ____.  | *maintain | shout | run
Reference sources ____ to avoid plagiarism. | *accurately | rarely | secretly
[*One limitation of the study is its short time frame.|Limitation study short time frame one.|Study limitation short frame.]
[*Furthermore, the data were collected in a single region.|Furthermore data collected single region.|Data furthermore single region.]
[*Nevertheless, the findings remain relevant to broader debates.|Nevertheless findings remain relevant broader.|Findings nevertheless relevant.]
[*To summarise, the benefits depend on how policies are designed.|Summarise benefits depend policies designed.|Benefits summarise depend designed.]
